title: Temporary residence for studies
userSituation: I study in Poland and need to understand temporary residence for studies.
summary: What the temporary residence permit for studies is, when it is relevant, and which official MOS pages describe the requirements and documents.
whoThisIsFor: Non-EU citizens studying in Poland or admitted to studies
Students whose visa is expiring and who are checking what a study-based residence permit is
People who keep seeing terms like "zezwolenie na pobyt czasowy" together with "studia"
commonDocuments: Application for a temporary residence permit for studies (submitted via MOS)
Certificate of admission or enrolment from the university
Documents confirming health insurance mentioned in official sources
Documents confirming financial means mentioned in official sources
whatThisExplains: What a temporary residence permit for the purpose of studies is
When this path is generally relevant
Which official MOS pages cover studies
How the study path differs from work- or family-based residence, at a high level
whatThisDoesNotDo: It does not tell you whether you qualify for a study permit
It does not check your enrolment, insurance, or financial documents
It does not calculate deadlines for your application
It does not replace the official MOS pages or advice from the office or your university
body:
Official sources describe a temporary residence permit granted for the purpose
of **studies** (*zezwolenie na pobyt czasowy w celu kształcenia się na
studiach*). It is the residence basis most often mentioned for non-EU citizens
whose main reason for staying in Poland is a full-time study programme.

The general idea in official sources: the education has to justify a stay
longer than 3 months, and the applicant is asked to document things like
**admission or enrolment at the university**, **health insurance**, and
**financial means** for living costs. The permit is decided by the voivode of
the region where you live, and applications are prepared through the official
MOS portal.

Studying is one of several residence bases. Working, joining family, and other
circumstances each have their own official path, with different document
lists. If your situation mixes several elements — for example you study and
also work — the official MOS pages describe which basis fits which situation,
and the office deciding your case makes the final call.

If an application is missing formal elements, the office sends a request for
filling formal gaps (*braki formalne*) with a deadline it sets. Because
requirements and lists change, always check the current official MOS pages
linked below.
